mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending recession risk, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth outpacing CPI inflation indicates healthy demand without overheating, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts positive returns but SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals supply chain normalization and higher capacity utilization, which expands corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises consecutively for three months but SPX 20-day forward returns remain negative, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts collapsing below 1 million units signals credit tightening and a sharp demand shock, which drags down consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST drops below 1000 but SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in the majority of cases, or the trigger yields a negative information coefficient over a 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates robust labor demand, driving consumer spending and supporting higher equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE remains flat but SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in over half of the observations, or the interaction effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPI increases erode real purchasing power and force prolonged monetary tightening, which depresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: CPI prints above 3% for three consecutive months but SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive, or the signal shows no directional accuracy over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and food services revenue directly reflect consumer demand strength, which translates to higher corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: RSAFS shows a positive month-over-month change but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances, or the signal fails to generate alpha after transaction costs.
